system: ideal gas equation of state without ensemble or fluctuation model

control_variables.name: chosen_ensemble
control_variables.meaning: thermodynamic ensemble required for a fluctuation object

control_variables.name: operating_point
control_variables.meaning: state point required for a local thermodynamic Hessian

regime_assumptions: gas is ideal if the equation of state is used
no ensemble or fluctuation model is declared
no local expansion around an operating point is declared
coordinate_note: The equation of state relates state variables but does not by itself declare a positive Hessian, covariance, Fisher object, or finite observer map.

module_object.description: no module object exists until an ensemble, thermodynamic potential, fluctuation model, or local measurement model is declared

derivation_status: not attachable from the equation of state alone
positive_scope: This card records the boundary that an equation of state is not automatically a module object.
boundary: It cannot call theorem-local quadratic or hidden-load surfaces without a declared thermodynamic potential, ensemble, fluctuation model, or local measurement route.
failure_mode: The attachment would overclaim if pV=nRT were treated as a Hessian or precision object by itself.
next_measurement: Choose a thermodynamic ensemble and potential, or declare a pressure/volume/temperature measurement equation with an uncertainty model.
